text:
Hello? Hello? is this thing on?
Are we live? Okay...

Good morning, people of America and
the wonderful listeners of NSBNC!

I'm Jeronica Wilkins, reporting live,
with what is the most consequential
interview of our time.

With me, inside of a computer screen,
is the President of our wonderful
country

Mr. President, I'll be blunt.
The people want answers.

Rumors are flying every which way,
theorizing about your whereabouts.

So, lets put those conspiracies to
rest. Where exactly are you right
now?

links.label: I am safe. That is all.
I am stuck in a bunker.

text: You are safe. How reassuring.

Forgive me, Mr. President, but
'I am safe' doesn't exactly
stop the presses.

Half this country hasn't slept
in three days. The other half
is stockpiling canned goods.

Saying you're safe tells us
where you are — but not what
you're doing there.

So let me ask it another way,
since we're being simple tonight.

Are the rumors true, sir?
Is the United States on the
brink of military conflict?

Can't disclose your location.
Naturally. I expected as much.
*leans into the microphone*

Though I must say, Mr. President,
the very fact that your location
is a secret speaks volumes.

In my twenty-two years behind
this microphone, I have never
had to interview a ghost.

The people filling up this
station's switchboard — they
don't need coordinates, sir.

They need to know their Commander
in Chief is in command.
Are you, Mr. President?

Are you in command of this
situation — whatever this
situation actually is?

links.label: We are closely monitoring the situation.
I have full confidence in my team.
